full_name: Ministry of Jobs and Economic Growth
blurb: Grows B.C.'s economy — business advisory services and financing, trade and investment attraction, technology and innovation support, and regional and rural economic development. Formerly the Ministry of Jobs, Economic Development and Innovation.

goals.title: GOAL 1
goals.short_name: British Columbians can start, grow and finance a successful business
goals.description: The ministry provides free advisory services, step-by-step guides, business support connections and financing tools — including tax credit and equity investment programs — to help entrepreneurs start, run and grow a business in B.C.

main_services.name: Start and get advice for your business

supporting.name: Get free business advisory services
supporting.desc: BC Business Advisory Services offers free one-on-one consultations, guidance on navigating government programs and funding, and resources to help entrepreneurs start, grow and thrive, in English and French.

supporting.name: Find the permits and licences your business needs
supporting.desc: BizPaL is a free online tool that generates a customized list of permits and licences a business may need to start or grow, based on its location and activities.

supporting.name: Follow step-by-step guidance to start your business
supporting.desc: A step-by-step guide covers evaluating a business idea, market research, business planning, choosing a business structure, registration, permits, tax registration and hiring employees.

supporting.name: Connect with a business support organization
supporting.desc: A directory of organizations offering advisory services, financing, mentorship and networking, including Community Futures, WeBC, Futurpreneur, the Black Business Association of BC, and support tailored to Indigenous, women, youth, newcomer and 2SLGBTQIA+ entrepreneurs.

supporting.name: Share feedback to make it easier to do business in B.C.
supporting.desc: The EaseBiz BC form lets businesses share the challenges and solutions they see, supporting the ministry's Ease of Doing Business initiative.

mandate_tags.tip: Work with business leaders to develop performance measurements on the ease of doing business in B.C. and address issues identified

main_services.name: Access capital and financing for your business

supporting.name: Get a tax credit for investing in a small business
supporting.desc: The Small Business Venture Capital Program offers tax credits to investors who make equity capital investments in registered venture capital corporations or eligible small businesses.

legislation.full_name: Small Business Venture Capital Act  -  Venture capital corporation and eligible business tax credits

supporting.name: Set up an employee share ownership plan
supporting.desc: The Employee Share Ownership Program gives employees a 20% tax credit for investing in their employer's business, and can help a retiring owner transfer the company to its employees.

legislation.full_name: Employee Investment Act  -  Registration and tax credits for employee share ownership plans

supporting.name: Get growth equity investment for your business
supporting.desc: InBC Investment Corp is a $500 million provincial strategic investment fund with a triple-bottom-line mandate that invests in B.C. businesses with high growth potential.

supporting.name: Prepare your business for economic disruption or disaster
supporting.desc: Guides help businesses prepare for and recover from disasters and broader economic shocks, including impacts from U.S. tariffs.

mandate_tags.tip: Review all existing ministry programs and initiatives to ensure they remain relevant, are efficient, grow the economy and help keep costs low given budget constraints and tariff threats

goals.title: GOAL 2
goals.short_name: B.C. businesses trade internationally and attract investment
goals.description: The ministry promotes B.C. internationally as a place to invest and do business, helps exporters diversify into new markets, and coordinates the province's response to U.S. tariffs and major project investment through the Look West strategy.

main_services.name: Grow your exports and international trade

supporting.name: Get help exporting your products to new markets
supporting.desc: The Export Navigator Program's community-based advisors provide free, ongoing guidance to help B.C. businesses grow their sales outside the province.
supporting.users: B.C. exporters and businesses new to export

supporting.name: Get coaching to grow your international trade
supporting.desc: The Trade Accelerator Program is a Canada-wide program offering strategic growth mentoring, skills training and coaching to help businesses expand into international trade.

supporting.name: Get help attracting investment or entering export markets
supporting.desc: Trade and Invest BC works with B.C. and international businesses to help attract foreign investment and find new markets for B.C. goods and services, including through representative offices abroad.

mandate_tags.tip: Lead work with the Parliamentary Secretary for Trade to enhance and expand B.C.'s Trade Diversification Strategy and diversify B.C.'s sources of international investment

supporting.name: Learn how trade agreements affect your business
supporting.desc: Information on B.C.'s trade and investment agreements and how to claim preferential tariff treatment in free trade agreement markets.

main_services.name: Attract investment and deliver major projects

supporting.name: Get support responding to U.S. tariffs
supporting.desc: Resources and the BC Tariff Response Committee support B.C. businesses and workers facing U.S. tariffs, alongside the Look West strategy's push to diversify export markets.
supporting.users: Businesses and workers affected by tariffs

channels.label: B.C.'s response to unjustified U.S. tariffs

mandate_tags.tip: Work with partners to direct B.C.'s response to U.S. tariffs and other unfair trade actions
Evolve the BC Tariff Response Committee to ensure continued integration with key partners in B.C.'s tariff response

supporting.name: Track major clean energy, mineral and natural gas projects in B.C.
supporting.desc: The Major Projects in B.C. website tracks accelerated clean energy, critical mineral, mining and natural gas projects the province is working to deliver faster as part of the Look West strategy.

channels.label: Major projects in B.C. delivered faster

mandate_tags.tip: Lead work with colleagues to accelerate major investments in B.C.'s economy that create well-paying jobs

supporting.name: Apply for B.C. Strategic Investment Fund financing
supporting.desc: The B.C. Strategic Investment Fund is a financing fund intended to encourage new investments in British Columbia; an application intake process is currently in development.

goals.title: GOAL 3
goals.short_name: B.C.'s technology, innovation and life sciences sectors grow
goals.description: Through Innovate BC, the B.C. Knowledge Development Fund and related programs, the ministry helps innovators and technology businesses access funding, research infrastructure and expert connections.

main_services.name: Get support to launch and grow a tech or innovation business

supporting.name: Get funding, mentorship and connections to grow your tech business
supporting.desc: Innovate BC, a provincial Crown agency, connects B.C. innovators with hiring grants, mentorship, research grants, pilot funding and innovation challenges.

mandate_tags.tip: Work with the Minister of State for Artificial Intelligence and New Technologies to expand B.C.'s AI and new technology sectors, including quantum and life sciences

supporting.name: Apply for research infrastructure funding
supporting.desc: The B.C. Knowledge Development Fund supports the development of research infrastructure like labs and equipment, helping institutions attract researchers and skilled technicians and collaborate with industry.

supporting.name: Get support to grow a life sciences or biomanufacturing business
supporting.desc: The Province supports expansion of B.C.'s life sciences and biomanufacturing sector, which already employs more than 20,000 people, as part of the Look West strategy's targeted sectors.

mandate_tags.tip: Work with the Minister of State for Artificial Intelligence and New Technologies to expand B.C.'s AI and new technology sectors, including quantum and life sciences

supporting.name: Get funding to improve workplace innovation and productivity
supporting.desc: The B.C. Workplace Innovation Fund supports projects that help B.C. businesses and organizations adopt innovative workplace practices and technology.

goals.title: GOAL 4
goals.short_name: Communities across B.C. build stronger local economies
goals.description: Regional economic development staff, funding programs and planning toolkits help local governments, Indigenous communities and businesses plan, fund and deliver economic development initiatives across urban and rural B.C.

main_services.name: Get funding and planning support for local economic development

supporting.name: Search for economic development funding and grants
supporting.desc: A searchable database connects local governments, Indigenous communities and businesses with funding opportunities for economic development initiatives.

supporting.name: Learn to plan and measure your community's economic development
supporting.desc: A Strategic Planning Toolkit and Performance Measurement Toolkit help communities learn the basics of economic development, build a plan, and measure the impact of their activities.
supporting.users: Local government and community economic development staff

channels.label: Learn the Basics, Plan and Measure Your Economic Development Efforts

supporting.name: Access Indigenous economic development resources
supporting.desc: Partnerships, tools and resources support First Nations and Indigenous communities pursuing their economic development goals under the StrongerBC Economic Plan.

supporting.name: Get support to recover from a major employer loss or economic shock
supporting.desc: Community Transition Services help communities plan and recover after the loss of a major employer or another significant economic disruption.

main_services.name: Get regional economic development and investment attraction support

supporting.name: Connect with a regional economic development manager
supporting.desc: Government economic development staff based across B.C.'s regions provide free advice on strategic planning, funding, business retention, land development and investment attraction, and support for local governments, Indigenous communities and businesses.
supporting.users: Local governments, Indigenous communities, businesses, economic development agencies

mandate_tags.tip: In partnership with the Parliamentary Secretary for Rural Development, ensure government initiatives consider and deliver economic growth and benefits for rural and urban communities

supporting.name: Run a business retention and expansion program in your community
supporting.desc: Tools help local governments and economic development organizations develop Business Retention and Expansion programs, organize Business Walks, and support business succession planning.

supporting.name: Attract investment and prepare land for development in your community
supporting.desc: A Business Attraction Toolkit and Land Development Toolkit help communities position themselves to attract new investment and assess their readiness to develop land parcels.

channels.label: Prepare for Business Investment and Land Development

mandate_tags.tip: Create an inventory of existing industrial lands, carry out a land-use needs assessment, and develop a strategy for protecting and growing the industrial land base

supporting.name: Apply a rural lens to policies affecting your community
supporting.desc: The Rural Initiative draft guidance helps ministries assess how provincial policies impact rural, remote and First Nations communities, which contribute over $84 billion annually to B.C.'s economy.

mandate_tags.tip: In partnership with the Parliamentary Secretary for Rural Development, ensure government initiatives consider and deliver economic growth and benefits for rural and urban communities

foundational_services.name: Economic and labour market research and statistics
foundational_services.desc: Collection and publication of economic data, major project tracking, and business and sector statistics that inform ministry programs and public research on B.C.'s economy.
foundational_services.users: Internal program staff, economic development practitioners

foundational_services.name: Trade and investment policy development and negotiation
foundational_services.desc: Policy analysis and negotiation support underlying B.C.'s trade agreements, trade diversification strategy and investment attraction work.

foundational_services.name: Major projects coordination and permitting support
foundational_services.desc: Cross-ministry coordination that helps accelerate permitting and approvals for major clean energy, critical mineral and natural gas projects under the Look West strategy.

mandate_legend.text: Review all existing Ministry of Jobs and Economic Growth programs and initiatives to ensure they remain relevant, are efficient, grow the economy and help keep costs low, given current provincial budget constraints and the threat of American tariffs
Work with partners to direct B.C.'s response to U.S. tariffs and other unfair trade actions
Lead work with colleagues to accelerate major investments in B.C.'s economy that create well-paying jobs
Support the development and implementation of policies that increase economic growth in B.C. to improve opportunities for all British Columbians
Evolve the BC Tariff Response Committee to ensure continued integration with key partners in B.C.'s tariff response
Lead work with the Parliamentary Secretary for Trade to enhance and expand B.C.'s Trade Diversification Strategy and diversify B.C.'s sources of international investment

Ensure proposed legislation or regulations are considered through the lens of impact on B.C. businesses, bringing forward reforms to support competitiveness while promoting high standards on human rights, environment and social well-being
Continue to diversify B.C.'s economy and support good jobs through the BC Manufacturing Jobs Fund
Work with business leaders to develop performance measurements on the ease of doing business in B.C. and address issues identified to improve competitiveness
Assume lead responsibility for the international film and television production tax credit program and related policy

Work with the Minister of State for Artificial Intelligence and New Technologies to expand B.C.'s AI and new technology sectors, including quantum and life sciences, and prepare workers for AI opportunities
Work with grocery stores and pharmacies to get more B.C. products on B.C. shelves
Continue to support small and medium-sized businesses to innovate, grow and access global markets
Advocate strongly to the federal government for fair treatment of B.C. businesses in relation to economic growth and stimulus programs
Create an inventory of existing industrial lands, carry out a land-use needs assessment, and develop a strategy for protecting and growing the industrial land base
Work with the Minister of Transportation and Transit to expand export infrastructure and improve access to industrial land, supporting B.C.'s role in facilitating trade with Asia
In partnership with the Parliamentary Secretary for Rural Development, ensure government initiatives consider and deliver economic growth and benefits for rural and urban communities
